Right, the person wants more information, or they may be schooled in selling and they want to test the salesperson. They want to see, well, they fold over. I do that. You know, what's this person made of? Or they have moxie. And I really think this is where salespeople that aren't schooled, that aren't trained, they really stumble because they think that it's a no. When it's really just a request for more information, or it's part of the romance, it's part of the sales process, and we need to get to the end result. Or it's them telling you that you haven't sold me. Yeah, I'm not sold yet. Yeah, you haven't sold me. And again, the thing is, there doesn't have to be this problem. Because if you know, there's a set of objections, which we're going to go over. And there's a set of objections that almost everybody gets. But then there's specific objections to what you're doing. Script them out. I mean, handle them, memorize them. I mean, that's what the pros do. That's what the pros do. You saw, I'll give you a two-critic example. So Ronald Reagan went, he ran, against Mondale. Most people won't remember Mondale. When Ronald Reagan ran against Mondale, there was the famous debate when Mondale rather foolishly brought up the age, you're old and decrepit and dangerous sort of objection, more elegantly than I just did, but still. And Reagan said he was on the successive answer with us four or five times. And I asked him about it. And he said, we really wish it had happened in the first debate, because I didn't do very well in the first debate. And we were waiting for this question. He would die in for it. Just die in for it, because we had a scripted zinger for it, which he turned to Mondale and then turned the public and said, I'm not going to hold this man's youth and inexperience against him. And everybody laughed, including Mondale. And Mondale was screwed. That was that point for him. It was like game over. So Carl and I are golden oaks, which is the big multi-million dollar home development that Disney is building. And we were there before there were even any finished models. So we're in a model that's like half done. And the back of it comes down in like four tiers of decking and foliage all the way down to the babbling brook, which is they put in, of course. So there's a deck for your patio. And then there's the spa and tub deck. And then there's the barbecue. And finally, there's a seating looking at the beautiful babbling brook, which Carl is kind of a water person. So she's excited. And I say, you don't spend that much time in Florida. I've spent a lot of time in Florida. You should know that four months out of the year, where there's water, there's mosquitoes, the size of buicks. And if you are going to sit out here, you're going to sit wrapped in a big net. And they're going to be bouncing off of it, coming at you. And the real estate person says, oh, Mr. Kennedy, we don't have mosquitoes at Disney. This is Disney. You're screwed. At that point, you're just done. This conversation is over. Done. All she has to do is have anticipated, memorized it, and practiced delivering it with a straight face. Because it's hard to deliver that line with a straight face. And so many times, it doesn't take much to make these things go away. People build up all sorts of negative anticipation and timidity and weakness about them. When really, it doesn't take that much to make them go away. Good. I think people assume when they come to an objection, there's a level of conflict. I mean, you were just making a statement. And she delivered it as best as it could be delivered. It was well done. She uses voice inflection, I'm sure. She touched you one of those five years. I mean, and we could all learn from that. And I think the salesperson will think that when a person just puts an objection out there, it is them saying no. Or it is not. It is just I need to keep on moving forward with the sales presentation. I realize this. I've heard it happen before. I've tested different objection re-bottles to that. And this is the one that works the best. And we're ready for the close after we achieve this objection. Yeah. And it's beautiful once you have it scripted, because like you were saying, like Reagan, that's why I wasn't kidding. You can actually look forward to getting the objection because it helps you move the sale forward. It's exactly right. It advances the sale so far. So far when this one is raised and you deal with it expertly, that it's the best thing that could possibly happen.
